why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.
seed_example: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'graph-interdiction' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'bakery supply chain'. Anchor idea: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.
seed_example: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'salmon migration ladder'. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.
seed_example: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'packing-knapsack' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (problems 0,1),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'vineyard irrigation'. Anchor idea: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

why_it_generalizes: TSP/VRP/pickup-delivery/Chinese-postman are the canonical construction-then-local-search family; transfers to any ordering/permutation objective.
seed_example: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'routing-tour' (tier S), inspired by ALE-Bench (ahc006/027/039),Frontier-CS. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'vaccine cold chain'. Anchor idea: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Job-shop / single-machine makespan & weighted-tardiness under precedence + no-overlap constraints teach dispatch-rule design and constraint feasibility that transfer to routing and assignment.
seed_example: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scheduling-jssp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-Eng (JobShop),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'mountain rescue relays'. Anchor idea: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).

why_it_generalizes: Generalized assignment / stable matching with costs generalizes bipartite structure to weighted, capacitated, preference-constrained settings.
seed_example: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'assignment-matching' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'salmon migration ladder'. Anchor idea: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Degree-constrained / Steiner-flavored connectivity design forces feasibility-gated cost minimization over spanning structures; the output-restriction mutation reliably yields NP-hardness.
seed_example: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'network-design' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'asteroid mining'. Anchor idea: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Max-cut / balanced partition / bisection reward move-based local search on a smooth objective; the archetype underlies clustering and coloring.
seed_example: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'partition-clustering' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench (partition flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'glacier sensor net'. Anchor idea: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Graph coloring / frequency assignment minimizing colors-or-conflicts teaches constraint-conflict minimization generalizing to CSP/Max-SAT.
seed_example: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'coloring-labeling' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'greenhouse zones'. Anchor idea: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.

why_it_generalizes: Weighted Max-SAT / general CSP is the universal constraint-optimization substrate; teaches soft-constraint weighting and randomized restarts.
seed_example: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'maxsat-csp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'orbital debris cleanup'. Anchor idea: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Max weighted independent set / feedback vertex set on general graphs is a canonical hard selection problem; the input-generalization mutation (bipartite->general) guarantees hardness.
seed_example: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'independent-set-clique' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,FunSearch (indep-set flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'satellite ground stations'. Anchor idea: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.

Anchor idea: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).

why_it_generalizes: Generalized assignment / stable matching with costs generalizes bipartite structure to weighted, capacitated, preference-constrained settings.
seed_example: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'assignment-matching' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'telescope array'. Anchor idea: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Degree-constrained / Steiner-flavored connectivity design forces feasibility-gated cost minimization over spanning structures; the output-restriction mutation reliably yields NP-hardness.
seed_example: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'network-design' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'volcano monitoring'. Anchor idea: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Max-cut / balanced partition / bisection reward move-based local search on a smooth objective; the archetype underlies clustering and coloring.
seed_example: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'partition-clustering' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench (partition flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'asteroid mining'. Anchor idea: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Graph coloring / frequency assignment minimizing colors-or-conflicts teaches constraint-conflict minimization generalizing to CSP/Max-SAT.
seed_example: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'coloring-labeling' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'vaccine cold chain'. Anchor idea: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.

why_it_generalizes: Weighted Max-SAT / general CSP is the universal constraint-optimization substrate; teaches soft-constraint weighting and randomized restarts.
seed_example: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'maxsat-csp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'wind tunnel sensors'. Anchor idea: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Max weighted independent set / feedback vertex set on general graphs is a canonical hard selection problem; the input-generalization mutation (bipartite->general) guarantees hardness.
seed_example: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'independent-set-clique' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,FunSearch (indep-set flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'volcano monitoring'. Anchor idea: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.
seed_example: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-to-offline-replay' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (cant_be_late offline),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'data-center cooling'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.
seed_example: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'graph-interdiction' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'polar research base'. Anchor idea: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.
seed_example: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
seed_example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).
brief: [C|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'greedy-priority-construction' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (cap-set/admissible/corner-free/cyclic-graph). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'beehive apiary'. Anchor idea: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

why_it_generalizes: Model writes a scoring function consumed by a FIXED streaming simulator (bin-packing priority(item,bins) -> -avg bins). Generalize by sampling instance distributions (uniform/Weibull, varying capacity/item-count) and reporting % excess over the L1 lower bound so sizes are comparable -> teaches online-algorithm design.
seed_example: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-heuristic-simulator' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (bin-packing),ALE-Bench (dispatch rules). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'carnival ride circuit'. Anchor idea: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.

why_it_generalizes: Emit coordinates+radii; an O(N^2) verifier checks containment+non-overlap then scores a continuous objective (sum of radii / min outer size). Parameterize by N, container shape, dimension -> a large family teaching constraint-respecting numeric construction + refinement.
seed_example: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'geometric-packing' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'orbital debris cleanup'. Anchor idea: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).

why_it_generalizes: Kissing-number integer point sets (max-norm < min pairwise distance), Heilbronn (maximize min triangle area), and min max/min pairwise-distance ratio all score deterministically from coordinates; parameterize by n and dimension d for many difficulty levels.
seed_example: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'extremal-point-config' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'glacier sensor net'. Anchor idea: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Represent an unknown function as an N-interval step function (or Hermite basis) and compute a bound via deterministic convolution/integration/root-finding. Covers autocorrelation C1/C2/C3, uncertainty, and Erdos minimum-overlap; vary N, which functional (L1/L2/Linf ratio), and min-vs-max direction.
seed_example: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'functional-inequality-constant' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'wildlife corridor'. Anchor idea: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.

why_it_generalizes: Emit an NxN +/-1 (or integer) matrix; verifier checks entries then computes EXACT integer determinant via Bareiss and scores |det|/theoretical_max. Exact integer arithmetic (no float tolerance); scales cleanly with N and matrix family (Hadamard/max-det/orthogonality-defect).
seed_example: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'integer-matrix-construction' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve,ThetaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'asteroid mining'. Anchor idea: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Build a finite integer set optimizing a closed-form arithmetic objective (sumset/difference-set exponent |A+A|/|A-A|, minimum-overlap). Purely arithmetic exact scoring, cheap, parameterizable by set-size budget.
seed_example: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'additive-combinatorics-set' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'festival stage layout'. Anchor idea: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Output a rank-R decomposition of a matrix-multiplication (or general small 3D) tensor; counter reconstructs the tensor (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) for an EXACT-equality gate, then score = R (# scalar multiplications). Parameterize over <m,n,p> with m,n,p<=5 and field (real/complex) -> the offline-safe surrogate for kernel FLOP reduction.
seed_example: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'tensor-decomposition-rank' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'satellite ground stations'. Anchor idea: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).

why_it_generalizes: Emit a point set; score = a deterministically computed geometric measure (star discrepancy / spherical-code min angle). Same construct-then-exactly-measure archetype as circle packing, scalable in size and dimension.
seed_example: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'low-discrepancy-pointset' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve (star-discrepancy),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'lunar habitat'. Anchor idea: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.

why_it_generalizes: AtCoder-heuristic-contest problems reframed as STATIC instances with a deterministic contest scorer under a FIXED iteration/op budget (not wall-time). Keeps the long-horizon objective-driven search while being reproducible offline; best-of-N/self-refine over the objective teaches iterative optimization.
seed_example: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'heuristic-contest-offline' (tier A), inspired by ALE-Bench (AHC),TTT-Discover (ahc). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'geothermal wells'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.

why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
seed_example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).
brief: [C|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'greedy-priority-construction' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (cap-set/admissible/corner-free/cyclic-graph). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #1, skinned as 'bakery supply chain'. Anchor idea: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'integer-matrix-construction' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve,ThetaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'wildlife corridor'. Anchor idea: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Build a finite integer set optimizing a closed-form arithmetic objective (sumset/difference-set exponent |A+A|/|A-A|, minimum-overlap). Purely arithmetic exact scoring, cheap, parameterizable by set-size budget.
seed_example: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'additive-combinatorics-set' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'satellite ground stations'. Anchor idea: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Output a rank-R decomposition of a matrix-multiplication (or general small 3D) tensor; counter reconstructs the tensor (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) for an EXACT-equality gate, then score = R (# scalar multiplications). Parameterize over <m,n,p> with m,n,p<=5 and field (real/complex) -> the offline-safe surrogate for kernel FLOP reduction.
seed_example: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'tensor-decomposition-rank' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'traffic signal grid'. Anchor idea: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).

why_it_generalizes: Emit a point set; score = a deterministically computed geometric measure (star discrepancy / spherical-code min angle). Same construct-then-exactly-measure archetype as circle packing, scalable in size and dimension.
seed_example: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'low-discrepancy-pointset' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve (star-discrepancy),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'bakery supply chain'. Anchor idea: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.

why_it_generalizes: AtCoder-heuristic-contest problems reframed as STATIC instances with a deterministic contest scorer under a FIXED iteration/op budget (not wall-time). Keeps the long-horizon objective-driven search while being reproducible offline; best-of-N/self-refine over the objective teaches iterative optimization.
seed_example: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'heuristic-contest-offline' (tier A), inspired by ALE-Bench (AHC),TTT-Discover (ahc). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'greenhouse zones'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.

why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
seed_example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).
brief: [C|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'greedy-priority-construction' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (cap-set/admissible/corner-free/cyclic-graph). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'reservoir dam network'. Anchor idea: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

why_it_generalizes: Model writes a scoring function consumed by a FIXED streaming simulator (bin-packing priority(item,bins) -> -avg bins). Generalize by sampling instance distributions (uniform/Weibull, varying capacity/item-count) and reporting % excess over the L1 lower bound so sizes are comparable -> teaches online-algorithm design.
seed_example: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-heuristic-simulator' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (bin-packing),ALE-Bench (dispatch rules). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'highway toll gantries'. Anchor idea: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.

why_it_generalizes: Emit coordinates+radii; an O(N^2) verifier checks containment+non-overlap then scores a continuous objective (sum of radii / min outer size). Parameterize by N, container shape, dimension -> a large family teaching constraint-respecting numeric construction + refinement.
seed_example: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'geometric-packing' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'data-center cooling'. Anchor idea: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).

why_it_generalizes: Kissing-number integer point sets (max-norm < min pairwise distance), Heilbronn (maximize min triangle area), and min max/min pairwise-distance ratio all score deterministically from coordinates; parameterize by n and dimension d for many difficulty levels.
seed_example: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'extremal-point-config' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'vaccine cold chain'. Anchor idea: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Represent an unknown function as an N-interval step function (or Hermite basis) and compute a bound via deterministic convolution/integration/root-finding. Covers autocorrelation C1/C2/C3, uncertainty, and Erdos minimum-overlap; vary N, which functional (L1/L2/Linf ratio), and min-vs-max direction.
seed_example: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'functional-inequality-constant' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'wind tunnel sensors'. Anchor idea: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.

Anchor idea: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.

why_it_generalizes: Minimize truss/tower weight or beam compliance subject to hard FEM stress/displacement/volume constraints (objective if feasible else +inf). Pure-numpy FEM on CPU; teaches 'find the best design within budget under a hard feasibility gate' -> the generative-optimization thesis.
seed_example: Choose bar cross-sections for a 45-bar 2D truss minimizing weight subject to stress+displacement limits; evaluator runs the numpy FEM and returns weight-if-feasible.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'feasibility-gated-structural' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (StructuralOptimization). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'mountain rescue relays'. Anchor idea: Choose bar cross-sections for a 45-bar 2D truss minimizing weight subject to stress+displacement limits; evaluator runs the numpy FEM and returns weight-if-feasible.

why_it_generalizes: Inventory (MEIO/joint-replenishment/EOQ) and portfolio MIP/CVaR scored by deterministic composite objectives normalized against baseline+reference; numpy/cvxpy CPU. Teaches constrained mixed-integer/convex trade-off search.
seed_example: Set safety-stock levels across a supply tree minimizing holding+shortage cost under a service-level constraint; evaluator computes the composite objective normalized to 100.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'constrained-OR' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (Inventory/Portfolio). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'deep-sea cable network'. Anchor idea: Set safety-stock levels across a supply tree minimizing holding+shortage cost under a service-level constraint; evaluator computes the composite objective normalized to 100.

why_it_generalizes: Adaptive-optics/holography efficiency & uniformity and importance-sampling BER/variance-reduction are deterministic-per-seed numpy objectives -> teaches iterative refinement on a continuous simulated reward without any hardware.
seed_example: Design a phase mask maximizing diffraction efficiency + uniformity of a target spot array; evaluator simulates propagation with a fixed seed and returns the composite metric.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'seeded-numerical-sim' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (Optics/Communications). Anchor idea: Design a phase mask maximizing diffraction efficiency + uniformity of a target spot array; evaluator simulates propagation with a fixed seed and returns the composite metric.

why_it_generalizes: Minimize gate/SWAP count and depth for QFT/QAOA/Clifford+T circuits on a named backend, verified by functional equivalence. A clean deterministic count-based reward -> the offline surrogate capturing kernel-like 'make the artifact cheaper' without timing.
seed_example: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'quantum-circuit-opcount' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (QuantumComputing),SimpleTES. Anchor idea: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.

why_it_generalizes: Fit a closed-form f(x) minimizing held-out MSE with an expression-complexity penalty. The train/held-out split forces the model to search expression space and GENERALIZE, not memorize points -> pure NumPy/SymPy deterministic score.
seed_example: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).
brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'symbolic-regression' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-CS (symbolic_regression),OpenEvolve. brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scientific-law-extrapolation' (tier B), inspired by SimpleTES (scaling laws). why_it_generalizes: Design a classical-ML algorithm (clustering/anomaly/calibration/boosting/imputation) scored by CPU-deterministic metrics (ARI/NMI/silhouette, AUC, ECE, R^2) via GEOMETRIC MEAN over several datasets -> gmean penalizes single-dataset overfit and rewards transferable method design.
seed_example: Design a clustering algorithm scored by gmean(ARI) over blobs/moons/digits; evaluator recomputes ARI deterministically.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'classical-ml-algorithm' (tier C), inspired by MLS-Bench (ml-*). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'warehouse robotics'. Anchor idea: Design a clustering algorithm scored by gmean(ARI) over blobs/moons/digits; evaluator recomputes ARI deterministically.

why_it_generalizes: Design a causal-discovery / treatment-effect rule scored by deterministic graph metrics (SHD, adjacency/arrow precision-recall, ATE error) across a battery of ground-truth graphs; multi-graph aggregate forces a generalizable discovery rule, CPU+seed-deterministic.
seed_example: Emit a discrete causal-discovery routine scored by mean SHD over Cancer/Child/Alarm/Hailfinder graphs.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'causal-discovery' (tier C), inspired by MLS-Bench (causal-*). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'telescope array'. Anchor idea: Emit a discrete causal-discovery routine scored by mean SHD over Cancer/Child/Alarm/Hailfinder graphs.

why_it_generalizes: Design an update/acquisition/bound scored by ITERATE-based deterministic quantities (final gradient norm, regret, variance-reduction ratio, PAC-Bayes bound, AUC of log-grad vs iteration) under an op/iteration budget rather than wall-time -> teaches efficient algorithm design without hardware coupling.
seed_example: Design an optimizer update for a convex-concave saddle problem minimizing final gradient norm after a fixed iteration budget; evaluator recomputes the norm deterministically.
brief: [B|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'optimization-convergence-analysis' (tier C), inspired by MLS-Bench (optimization-*). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'deep-sea cable network'. Anchor idea: Design an optimizer update for a convex-concave saddle problem minimizing final gradient norm after a fixed iteration budget; evaluator recomputes the norm deterministically.

why_it_generalizes: Copy/reverse/Dyck/counting/associative-recall/modular-addition/sparse-parity with an explicit IN-DISTRIBUTION vs LENGTH-OOD split, tiny CPU models, deterministic exact-match. The ID-vs-OOD gap directly rewards generalization over memorization -> the purest generalization signal.
seed_example: Design the training tweak for a tiny transformer on Dyck-matching; score = gmean(exact_match) on ID plus longer-OOD sequences.
brief: [B|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'synthetic-algorithmic-generalization' (tier C), inspired by MLS-Bench (synth-*). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'drone delivery swarm'. Anchor idea: Design the training tweak for a tiny transformer on Dyck-matching; score = gmean(exact_match) on ID plus longer-OOD sequences.

why_it_generalizes: Edit ONE small component (activation/normalization/optimizer update/loss/LR schedule/init) on CPU-cheap proxies, scored by gmean of a deterministic metric over >=3 settings PLUS a HIDDEN held-out setting -> teaches transferable atomic ML-method invention, not per-dataset tuning. (Only CPU-cheap seed-deterministic proxies; exclude GPU-trained variants.)
seed_example: Design a drop-in activation function scored by gmean(test acc) over three small MLP/CNN settings plus one hidden setting, all CPU-trainable to convergence with fixed seeds.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'modular-component-cross-setting' (tier C), inspired by MLS-Bench (dl-*/llm-*, CPU proxies only). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #0, skinned as 'glacier sensor net'. Anchor idea: Design a drop-in activation function scored by gmean(test acc) over three small MLP/CNN settings plus one hidden setting, all CPU-trainable to convergence with fixed seeds.

why_it_generalizes: Latin-square/block-design/covering-code completion, matrix-rank/determinant-defect construction, minimal-automaton-size, and reconfiguration move-count are exotic but share the construct-and-exactly-measure shape -> broadens coverage so the model does not overfit the mainstream families.
seed_example: Complete a partially filled Latin square maximizing the number of validly filled cells; verifier checks row/column constraints exactly and scores filled-cell count / baseline.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'algebraic-exotic-construction' (tier C), inspired by AlphaEvolve,Frontier-CS. why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.

Anchor idea: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.

Anchor idea: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.

brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.

brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'packing-knapsack' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (problems 0,1),AlphaEvolve. Anchor idea: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

Vary it to a 'large'-scale instance, variant #8, skinned as 'coral reef survey'. Anchor idea: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.

why_it_generalizes: Weighted Max-SAT / general CSP is the universal constraint-optimization substrate; teaches soft-constraint weighting and randomized restarts.
seed_example: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'maxsat-csp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench. Vary it to a 'large'-scale instance, variant #8, skinned as 'cave mapping expedition'. Anchor idea: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Max weighted independent set / feedback vertex set on general graphs is a canonical hard selection problem; the input-generalization mutation (bipartite->general) guarantees hardness.
seed_example: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'independent-set-clique' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,FunSearch (indep-set flavor). Vary it to a 'large'-scale instance, variant #8, skinned as 'subway system'. Anchor idea: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.
seed_example: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-to-offline-replay' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (cant_be_late offline),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'large'-scale instance, variant #8, skinned as 'ski resort lifts'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.
seed_example: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'graph-interdiction' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'museum gallery tour'. Anchor idea: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.
seed_example: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'ski resort lifts'. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.
seed_example: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'packing-knapsack' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (problems 0,1),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'traffic signal grid'. Anchor idea: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

why_it_generalizes: TSP/VRP/pickup-delivery/Chinese-postman are the canonical construction-then-local-search family; transfers to any ordering/permutation objective.
seed_example: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'routing-tour' (tier S), inspired by ALE-Bench (ahc006/027/039),Frontier-CS. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'reservoir dam network'. Anchor idea: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Job-shop / single-machine makespan & weighted-tardiness under precedence + no-overlap constraints teach dispatch-rule design and constraint feasibility that transfer to routing and assignment.
seed_example: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scheduling-jssp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-Eng (JobShop),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'telescope array'. Anchor idea: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).

why_it_generalizes: Generalized assignment / stable matching with costs generalizes bipartite structure to weighted, capacitated, preference-constrained settings.
seed_example: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'assignment-matching' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #9, skinned as 'warehouse robotics'. Anchor idea: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Degree-constrained / Steiner-flavored connectivity design forces feasibility-gated cost minimization over spanning structures; the output-restriction mutation reliably yields NP-hardness.
seed_example: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'network-design' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Anchor idea: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.

brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'interstellar relay'. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.
seed_example: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'packing-knapsack' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (problems 0,1),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'beehive apiary'. Anchor idea: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

why_it_generalizes: TSP/VRP/pickup-delivery/Chinese-postman are the canonical construction-then-local-search family; transfers to any ordering/permutation objective.
seed_example: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'routing-tour' (tier S), inspired by ALE-Bench (ahc006/027/039),Frontier-CS. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'asteroid mining'. Anchor idea: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Job-shop / single-machine makespan & weighted-tardiness under precedence + no-overlap constraints teach dispatch-rule design and constraint feasibility that transfer to routing and assignment.
seed_example: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scheduling-jssp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-Eng (JobShop),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'traffic signal grid'. Anchor idea: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).

why_it_generalizes: Generalized assignment / stable matching with costs generalizes bipartite structure to weighted, capacitated, preference-constrained settings.
seed_example: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'assignment-matching' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'interstellar relay'. Anchor idea: Assign drones to delivery zones under per-drone capacity to maximize served weight; checker validates capacities and scores total value / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Degree-constrained / Steiner-flavored connectivity design forces feasibility-gated cost minimization over spanning structures; the output-restriction mutation reliably yields NP-hardness.
seed_example: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'network-design' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'lunar habitat'. Anchor idea: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Max-cut / balanced partition / bisection reward move-based local search on a smooth objective; the archetype underlies clustering and coloring.
seed_example: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'partition-clustering' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench (partition flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'polar research base'. Anchor idea: Partition a weighted graph into two balanced halves maximizing cut weight; checker validates balance and scores cut/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Graph coloring / frequency assignment minimizing colors-or-conflicts teaches constraint-conflict minimization generalizing to CSP/Max-SAT.
seed_example: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'coloring-labeling' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'satellite ground stations'. Anchor idea: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.

why_it_generalizes: Weighted Max-SAT / general CSP is the universal constraint-optimization substrate; teaches soft-constraint weighting and randomized restarts.
seed_example: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'maxsat-csp' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,MLS-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'mountain rescue relays'. Anchor idea: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Max weighted independent set / feedback vertex set on general graphs is a canonical hard selection problem; the input-generalization mutation (bipartite->general) guarantees hardness.
seed_example: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'independent-set-clique' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,FunSearch (indep-set flavor). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'glacier sensor net'. Anchor idea: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight/baseline.

why_it_generalizes: Replaying online problems (paging, interval selection, spot-vs-on-demand) as STATIC full-trace optimization removes timing while keeping the sequential-decision structure -> teaches lookahead policy design deterministically.
seed_example: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-to-offline-replay' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (cant_be_late offline),ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'volcano monitoring'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.

why_it_generalizes: Destructive optimization under a budget (remove k edges/nodes to worsen a network metric) forces constraint-respecting search over an exponential choice space with a clean validity gate; core NP-hard skill reused in scheduling/covering.
seed_example: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'graph-interdiction' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,ALE-Bench. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'reservoir dam network'. Anchor idea: Given a themed grid network, remove at most k links to MAXIMIZE the increase in s-t shortest path; checker verifies budget + connectivity and scores the achieved delay ratio.

why_it_generalizes: Set/vertex cover, k-center, dominating set share a 'cover all demand with fewest/cheapest resources' structure -> teaches greedy-vs-LP-rounding trade-offs generalizing to allocation and scheduling.
seed_example: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'covering-facility-location' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS,Frontier-Eng. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'glacier sensor net'. Anchor idea: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.

why_it_generalizes: Multi-dimensional bounded knapsack and polyomino/area packing reward value-density heuristics + local search under hard capacity constraints; the packing skeleton recurs in geometry and scheduling.
seed_example: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'packing-knapsack' (tier S), inspired by Frontier-CS (problems 0,1),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'volcano monitoring'. Anchor idea: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.

why_it_generalizes: TSP/VRP/pickup-delivery/Chinese-postman are the canonical construction-then-local-search family; transfers to any ordering/permutation objective.
seed_example: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.
brief: [A|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'routing-tour' (tier S), inspired by ALE-Bench (ahc006/027/039),Frontier-CS. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'carnival ride circuit'. Anchor idea: Choose which pickup-delivery pairs to serve and output one depot-to-depot tour minimizing distance; checker validates precedence + returns tour length ratio.

why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
seed_example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).
brief: [C|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'greedy-priority-construction' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (cap-set/admissible/corner-free/cyclic-graph). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'polar research base'. Anchor idea: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

why_it_generalizes: Model writes a scoring function consumed by a FIXED streaming simulator (bin-packing priority(item,bins) -> -avg bins). Generalize by sampling instance distributions (uniform/Weibull, varying capacity/item-count) and reporting % excess over the L1 lower bound so sizes are comparable -> teaches online-algorithm design.
seed_example: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-heuristic-simulator' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (bin-packing),ALE-Bench (dispatch rules). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'recycling depot routes'. Anchor idea: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.

why_it_generalizes: Emit coordinates+radii; an O(N^2) verifier checks containment+non-overlap then scores a continuous objective (sum of radii / min outer size). Parameterize by N, container shape, dimension -> a large family teaching constraint-respecting numeric construction + refinement.
seed_example: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'geometric-packing' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'quantum lab wiring'. Anchor idea: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).

why_it_generalizes: Kissing-number integer point sets (max-norm < min pairwise distance), Heilbronn (maximize min triangle area), and min max/min pairwise-distance ratio all score deterministically from coordinates; parameterize by n and dimension d for many difficulty levels.
seed_example: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'extremal-point-config' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'ski resort lifts'. Anchor idea: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Represent an unknown function as an N-interval step function (or Hermite basis) and compute a bound via deterministic convolution/integration/root-finding. Covers autocorrelation C1/C2/C3, uncertainty, and Erdos minimum-overlap; vary N, which functional (L1/L2/Linf ratio), and min-vs-max direction.
seed_example: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'functional-inequality-constant' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'mountain rescue relays'. Anchor idea: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.

why_it_generalizes: Emit an NxN +/-1 (or integer) matrix; verifier checks entries then computes EXACT integer determinant via Bareiss and scores |det|/theoretical_max. Exact integer arithmetic (no float tolerance); scales cleanly with N and matrix family (Hadamard/max-det/orthogonality-defect).
seed_example: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'integer-matrix-construction' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve,ThetaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'warehouse robotics'. Anchor idea: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Build a finite integer set optimizing a closed-form arithmetic objective (sumset/difference-set exponent |A+A|/|A-A|, minimum-overlap). Purely arithmetic exact scoring, cheap, parameterizable by set-size budget.
seed_example: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'additive-combinatorics-set' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'wind tunnel sensors'. Anchor idea: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Output a rank-R decomposition of a matrix-multiplication (or general small 3D) tensor; counter reconstructs the tensor (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) for an EXACT-equality gate, then score = R (# scalar multiplications). Parameterize over <m,n,p> with m,n,p<=5 and field (real/complex) -> the offline-safe surrogate for kernel FLOP reduction.
seed_example: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'tensor-decomposition-rank' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'e-sports arena'. Anchor idea: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).

why_it_generalizes: Emit a point set; score = a deterministically computed geometric measure (star discrepancy / spherical-code min angle). Same construct-then-exactly-measure archetype as circle packing, scalable in size and dimension.
seed_example: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'low-discrepancy-pointset' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve (star-discrepancy),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'carnival ride circuit'. Anchor idea: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.

why_it_generalizes: AtCoder-heuristic-contest problems reframed as STATIC instances with a deterministic contest scorer under a FIXED iteration/op budget (not wall-time). Keeps the long-horizon objective-driven search while being reproducible offline; best-of-N/self-refine over the objective teaches iterative optimization.
seed_example: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'heuristic-contest-offline' (tier A), inspired by ALE-Bench (AHC),TTT-Discover (ahc). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #7, skinned as 'forest fire watchtowers'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.
