Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'drone delivery swarm'. Anchor idea: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Build a finite integer set optimizing a closed-form arithmetic objective (sumset/difference-set exponent |A+A|/|A-A|, minimum-overlap). Purely arithmetic exact scoring, cheap, parameterizable by set-size budget.
seed_example: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'additive-combinatorics-set' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'quantum lab wiring'. Anchor idea: Construct an integer set A of size <=200 maximizing |A+A|/|A-A|; verifier computes both sumsets exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Output a rank-R decomposition of a matrix-multiplication (or general small 3D) tensor; counter reconstructs the tensor (entries rounded to nearest integer/half-integer) for an EXACT-equality gate, then score = R (# scalar multiplications). Parameterize over <m,n,p> with m,n,p<=5 and field (real/complex) -> the offline-safe surrogate for kernel FLOP reduction.
seed_example: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'tensor-decomposition-rank' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'harbor container port'. Anchor idea: Find a rank-R decomposition of the <3,3,3> matmul tensor over the rationals; counter verifies exact reconstruction and reports R (lower is better).

why_it_generalizes: Emit a point set; score = a deterministically computed geometric measure (star discrepancy / spherical-code min angle). Same construct-then-exactly-measure archetype as circle packing, scalable in size and dimension.
seed_example: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'low-discrepancy-pointset' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve (star-discrepancy),AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'tide pool ecology'. Anchor idea: Emit M points in [0,1]^d minimizing star discrepancy; verifier computes the discrepancy exactly over the induced boxes.

why_it_generalizes: AtCoder-heuristic-contest problems reframed as STATIC instances with a deterministic contest scorer under a FIXED iteration/op budget (not wall-time). Keeps the long-horizon objective-driven search while being reproducible offline; best-of-N/self-refine over the objective teaches iterative optimization.
seed_example: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'heuristic-contest-offline' (tier A), inspired by ALE-Bench (AHC),TTT-Discover (ahc). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #12, skinned as 'data-center cooling'. Anchor idea: Given a fixed fishing-region instance, output a polygon selection scored by the official deterministic contest formula under a 1e7-operation budget enforced by the harness.

why_it_generalizes: The single FunSearch template 'evolve priority(element, params); a frozen scaffold greedily builds a valid set; score = cardinality' covers cap sets, admissible sets, corner-free sets, and independent sets in graph products. Batch-generate by varying (n,w,num_nodes,power,constraint-family) so the model learns 'design a good ordering heuristic under a validity checker', not one construction.
seed_example: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).
brief: [C|correctness] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'greedy-priority-construction' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (cap-set/admissible/corner-free/cyclic-graph). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'deep-sea cable network'. Anchor idea: Emit a priority over F_3^n vectors; scaffold greedily builds a cap set (no 3 collinear); score = |set| for n=4..7 (small=fast reward, large=eval).

why_it_generalizes: Model writes a scoring function consumed by a FIXED streaming simulator (bin-packing priority(item,bins) -> -avg bins). Generalize by sampling instance distributions (uniform/Weibull, varying capacity/item-count) and reporting % excess over the L1 lower bound so sizes are comparable -> teaches online-algorithm design.
seed_example: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'online-heuristic-simulator' (tier A), inspired by FunSearch (bin-packing),ALE-Bench (dispatch rules). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'power grid'. Anchor idea: Evolve priority(item, bins) for online 1D bin packing; simulator places each arrival in the highest-priority valid bin; score = -mean(bins) as % over L1 bound across an instance family.

why_it_generalizes: Emit coordinates+radii; an O(N^2) verifier checks containment+non-overlap then scores a continuous objective (sum of radii / min outer size). Parameterize by N, container shape, dimension -> a large family teaching constraint-respecting numeric construction + refinement.
seed_example: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'geometric-packing' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'aquarium plumbing'. Anchor idea: Place N circles in the unit square maximizing sum of radii (N=10..40); verify.py checks 1e-6 non-overlap+containment, score=sum(r).

why_it_generalizes: Kissing-number integer point sets (max-norm < min pairwise distance), Heilbronn (maximize min triangle area), and min max/min pairwise-distance ratio all score deterministically from coordinates; parameterize by n and dimension d for many difficulty levels.
seed_example: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'extremal-point-config' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'quantum lab wiring'. Anchor idea: Place 11 points in the unit triangle maximizing the minimum triangle area over all triples; verifier computes the min area exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Represent an unknown function as an N-interval step function (or Hermite basis) and compute a bound via deterministic convolution/integration/root-finding. Covers autocorrelation C1/C2/C3, uncertainty, and Erdos minimum-overlap; vary N, which functional (L1/L2/Linf ratio), and min-vs-max direction.
seed_example: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'functional-inequality-constant' (tier A), inspired by AlphaEvolve,OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'data-center cooling'. Anchor idea: Emit a non-negative step sequence f; score c1 = 2n*max(conv(f,f))/sum(f)^2 to MINIMIZE (first autocorrelation inequality), vary n_steps.

why_it_generalizes: Emit an NxN +/-1 (or integer) matrix; verifier checks entries then computes EXACT integer determinant via Bareiss and scores |det|/theoretical_max. Exact integer arithmetic (no float tolerance); scales cleanly with N and matrix family (Hadamard/max-det/orthogonality-defect).
seed_example: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.
brief: [C|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'integer-matrix-construction' (tier A), inspired by OpenEvolve,ThetaEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #13, skinned as 'traffic signal grid'. Anchor idea: Construct a 20x20 +/-1 matrix maximizing |det|/20^10; verify.py runs Bareiss exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Maximize Pareto hypervolume under a FIXED evaluation budget on a deterministic surrogate; teaches budgeted multi-objective trade-off search, deterministic given seeds+budget.
seed_example: Propose a batch of query points on the DTLZ2 surface maximizing hypervolume under a 50-evaluation budget; evaluator computes hypervolume exactly.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'multiobjective-hypervolume' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (ReactionOptimisation/DTLZ2). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'solar farm inverters'. Anchor idea: Propose a batch of query points on the DTLZ2 surface maximizing hypervolume under a 50-evaluation budget; evaluator computes hypervolume exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Deterministic simulated-cost policies over fixed traces/tables: spot-vs-on-demand scheduling, multi-cloud broadcast cost, cost-aware model routing, KV-cache column ordering. No timing; teaches offline sequential decision + closed-form cost minimization that generalizes to unseen traces.
seed_example: Reorder SQL predicate columns to maximize prefix KV-cache hit-rate; evaluator computes (hit_rate-baseline)/(1-baseline)*100 on fixed data (the 10s cap is only a gate).
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'offline-decision-policy' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-CS (cant_be_late/cloudcast/llm_router/llm_sql). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #3, skinned as 'greenhouse zones'. Anchor idea: Reorder SQL predicate columns to maximize prefix KV-cache hit-rate; evaluator computes (hit_rate-baseline)/(1-baseline)*100 on fixed data (the 10s cap is only a gate).

why_it_generalizes: Minimize truss/tower weight or beam compliance subject to hard FEM stress/displacement/volume constraints (objective if feasible else +inf). Pure-numpy FEM on CPU; teaches 'find the best design within budget under a hard feasibility gate' -> the generative-optimization thesis.
seed_example: Choose bar cross-sections for a 45-bar 2D truss minimizing weight subject to stress+displacement limits; evaluator runs the numpy FEM and returns weight-if-feasible.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'feasibility-gated-structural' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (StructuralOptimization). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'aquarium plumbing'. Anchor idea: Choose bar cross-sections for a 45-bar 2D truss minimizing weight subject to stress+displacement limits; evaluator runs the numpy FEM and returns weight-if-feasible.

why_it_generalizes: Inventory (MEIO/joint-replenishment/EOQ) and portfolio MIP/CVaR scored by deterministic composite objectives normalized against baseline+reference; numpy/cvxpy CPU. Teaches constrained mixed-integer/convex trade-off search.
seed_example: Set safety-stock levels across a supply tree minimizing holding+shortage cost under a service-level constraint; evaluator computes the composite objective normalized to 100.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'constrained-OR' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (Inventory/Portfolio). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'coral reef survey'. Anchor idea: Set safety-stock levels across a supply tree minimizing holding+shortage cost under a service-level constraint; evaluator computes the composite objective normalized to 100.

why_it_generalizes: Adaptive-optics/holography efficiency & uniformity and importance-sampling BER/variance-reduction are deterministic-per-seed numpy objectives -> teaches iterative refinement on a continuous simulated reward without any hardware.
seed_example: Design a phase mask maximizing diffraction efficiency + uniformity of a target spot array; evaluator simulates propagation with a fixed seed and returns the composite metric.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'seeded-numerical-sim' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (Optics/Communications). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'forest fire watchtowers'. Anchor idea: Design a phase mask maximizing diffraction efficiency + uniformity of a target spot array; evaluator simulates propagation with a fixed seed and returns the composite metric.

why_it_generalizes: Minimize gate/SWAP count and depth for QFT/QAOA/Clifford+T circuits on a named backend, verified by functional equivalence. A clean deterministic count-based reward -> the offline surrogate capturing kernel-like 'make the artifact cheaper' without timing.
seed_example: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.
brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'quantum-circuit-opcount' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (QuantumComputing),SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'vaccine cold chain'. Anchor idea: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.

why_it_generalizes: Fit a closed-form f(x) minimizing held-out MSE with an expression-complexity penalty. The train/held-out split forces the model to search expression space and GENERALIZE, not memorize points -> pure NumPy/SymPy deterministic score.
seed_example: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).
brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'symbolic-regression' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-CS (symbolic_regression),OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'archaeology dig grid'. Anchor idea: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).

why_it_generalizes: Derive a scaling-law functional form and fit it, scored by EXTRAPOLATION error on a held-out region. Deterministic CPU regression; the natural train/unseen axis directly measures generalization vs overfit.
seed_example: Given a (compute, loss) table, propose+fit a parametric scaling law; score = extrapolation error at held-out larger-compute points.
brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scientific-law-extrapolation' (tier B), inspired by SimpleTES (scaling laws). Vary it to a 'medium'-scale instance, variant #4, skinned as 'rooftop gardens'. Anchor idea: Given a (compute, loss) table, propose+fit a parametric scaling law; score = extrapolation error at held-out larger-compute points.

brief: [D|flops] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'quantum-circuit-opcount' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (QuantumComputing),SimpleTES. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #6, skinned as 'reservoir dam network'. Anchor idea: Emit a routing/transpilation for a QAOA circuit on the IBM Falcon coupling map minimizing added SWAP gates; counter checks equivalence and returns the SWAP count.

why_it_generalizes: Fit a closed-form f(x) minimizing held-out MSE with an expression-complexity penalty. The train/held-out split forces the model to search expression space and GENERALIZE, not memorize points -> pure NumPy/SymPy deterministic score.
seed_example: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).
brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'symbolic-regression' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-CS (symbolic_regression),OpenEvolve. Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #6, skinned as 'bakery supply chain'. Anchor idea: Fit a closed-form expression to sampled points from a hidden 4-D poly-exp formula; score = f(heldout R^2, complexity).

why_it_generalizes: Derive a scaling-law functional form and fit it, scored by EXTRAPOLATION error on a held-out region. Deterministic CPU regression; the natural train/unseen axis directly measures generalization vs overfit.
seed_example: Given a (compute, loss) table, propose+fit a parametric scaling law; score = extrapolation error at held-out larger-compute points.
brief: [E|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'scientific-law-extrapolation' (tier B), inspired by SimpleTES (scaling laws). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #6, skinned as 'wind tunnel sensors'. Anchor idea: Given a (compute, loss) table, propose+fit a parametric scaling law; score = extrapolation error at held-out larger-compute points.

why_it_generalizes: Maximize Pareto hypervolume under a FIXED evaluation budget on a deterministic surrogate; teaches budgeted multi-objective trade-off search, deterministic given seeds+budget.
seed_example: Propose a batch of query points on the DTLZ2 surface maximizing hypervolume under a 50-evaluation budget; evaluator computes hypervolume exactly.
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'multiobjective-hypervolume' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-Eng (ReactionOptimisation/DTLZ2). Vary it to a 'small'-scale instance, variant #6, skinned as 'harbor container port'. Anchor idea: Propose a batch of query points on the DTLZ2 surface maximizing hypervolume under a 50-evaluation budget; evaluator computes hypervolume exactly.

why_it_generalizes: Deterministic simulated-cost policies over fixed traces/tables: spot-vs-on-demand scheduling, multi-cloud broadcast cost, cost-aware model routing, KV-cache column ordering. No timing; teaches offline sequential decision + closed-form cost minimization that generalizes to unseen traces.
seed_example: Reorder SQL predicate columns to maximize prefix KV-cache hit-rate; evaluator computes (hit_rate-baseline)/(1-baseline)*100 on fixed data (the 10s cap is only a gate).
brief: [B|quality-metric] Instantiate a NOVEL, deterministically-scored problem in family 'offline-decision-policy' (tier B), inspired by Frontier-CS (cant_be_late/cloudcast/llm_router/llm_sql). Anchor idea: Reorder SQL predicate columns to maximize prefix KV-cache hit-rate; evaluator computes (hit_rate-baseline)/(1-baseline)*100 on fixed data (the 10s cap is only a gate).

why_it_generalizes: composite objective + mathematical twist forces multi-mechanism reasoning
seed_example: Remove <=k links (budget) so that EVERY surviving path from source s to sink t has length that is COMPOSITE (never prime); minimize (removal cost + a penalty per surviving prime-length s-t path). Composite: interdiction budget x number-theoretic path-length constraint. gen must plant instances where the greedy 'cut the shortest path' is a trap.
brief: [A|BESPOKE-NOVELTY] Author a NOVEL, DEEP, deterministically-scored testlib problem in family 'graph-interdiction-primality', objective=minimize, skin 'quantum lab wiring'. This must be a TOP-tier bespoke problem, NOT a textbook NP-hard core in a skin. Core idea: Remove <=k links (budget) so that EVERY surviving path from source s to sink t has length that is COMPOSITE (never prime); minimize (removal cost + a penalty per surviving prime-length s-t path). Composite: interdiction budget x number-theoretic path-length constraint. gen must plant instances where the greedy 'cut the shortest path' is a trap. Follow the NOVELTY section of the brief (composite objective, the math twist, non-linear coupled scoring, and an ADVERSARIAL generator with planted/trap/needle cases that FILLS the stated constraint envelope on the largest tests).

seed_example: Over T rounds, each round pick sensors (paying per activation); coverage a sensor gives DECAYS each round it stays on and CARRIES OVER when off.
